profile.name: Tobi Awolaju
profile.title: Web3 Systems Engineer • Smart Contract Engineer • AI Agent Builder
profile.bio: I build production-grade Web3 systems—from smart contracts and AI agents to blockchain infrastructure and real-time multiplayer applications. My work spans protocol research platforms, on-chain accountability systems, multiplayer networking, and blockchain indexing. I enjoy solving distributed systems problems that sit between backend infrastructure, cryptography, and user experience.

projects.title: KYP
projects.description: Research protocols. Commit with a stake. Let your wallet prove you followed through.

writeup.hardPart: Building a protocol-agnostic verification system that works across any EVM protocol without relying on custom event signatures. I designed an architecture that scans a user's on-chain activity using indexed log queries, filters engagement by protocol contract, and automatically resolves commitments through Solidity contracts and a heartbeat-driven backend worker. The same architecture supports both Monad Testnet and Mainnet while remaining chain-agnostic.
highlights: Smart contract commitment system with automatic verify/slash
Zero-cost heartbeat worker instead of paid cron services
Adaptive block-time estimation instead of hardcoded assumptions
Verified worker test harness covering all commitment paths
writeup.stack: Svelte 5, TypeScript, Node.js, Solidity, viem, ethers, Firebase RTDB, Gemini, Privy, Monad.

projects.title: World of Nads ↗
projects.description: 20-player browser battle royale — 3D assets, a 20Hz WebSocket server keeping every player in sync in real time, on-chain prize distribution, and embedded wallet onboarding. No install, runs in-browser.

writeup.hardPart: Keeping 20 players' state perfectly synced over WebSocket while the frontend also has to talk to a wallet layer and on-chain state — three moving pieces that all had to stay consistent under real network jitter. I profiled and fixed a bottleneck where per-frame calls across the Godot/React bridge were tanking browser FPS from 60 to under 12, then rebuilt the sync path around batched state updates.
writeup.stack: Godot (WASM), TypeScript, React, Node.js WebSocket server, Solidity, PostgreSQL, Privy.

projects.title: Rug Pool ↗
projects.description: A memecoin launchpad where no one — not even the deployer — can exit early. On-chain, verifiable randomness decides who exits at the 24-hour mark; losers fund the winner's payout.

writeup.hardPart: Enforcing a hard lock at the contract level with no admin backdoor, while making the randomness both unpredictable and independently verifiable. Used Pyth Entropy for VRF. Shipped 5 contracts to Monad testnet with 53 passing tests and ran two full simulation cycles to confirm the payout logic held under edge cases.

otherBuilds.title: MoyakiTraceEngine
otherBuilds.description: A block indexer built for chains that reorganize. Cross-references three independent Monad nodes in real time and automatically rolls back state on parent-hash mismatch.

docs.title: Success is 90% luck and informed guessing. Success is 100% luck if you're uninformed. ↗
